faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about FPMI Courses

whatIs.a: FPMI Courses is a platform created by students for students at the Faculty of Applied Mathematics and Informatics (FPMI) at the Technical University of Sofia. It offers collaboratively edited notes, wiki pages for courses, assignments, resources, past exams, and discussion forums.

whoCanUse.a: Anyone can browse content as a guest. Students can register to create and edit wiki pages, post in forums, and submit reports or suggestions.

howContribute.a: Simply register and open any wiki page for editing. Make your changes, add a brief summary (what and why you changed), and submit. Your edit will be reviewed by a moderator before being published.

howApproval.a: When you edit a page, the new version awaits moderator approval. The moderator reviews the diff between the old and new version, then can approve or reject the change. This ensures content quality while allowing any student to contribute.

whatIsWiki.a: Wiki pages are the primary way to write notes for courses. They support Markdown formatting with the ability to add tables, code, images, and links.

whatCourses.a: The platform covers courses from FPMI specialties, grouped into categories: Programming, Mathematics, Data Analytics, AI, Databases, Networks, and Cybersecurity.

hasForum.a: Yes, each course has its own forum where you can ask questions, discuss materials, and help each other. Each wiki page has a "Discuss" button that leads to the corresponding forum thread.

howReport.q: How can I report an issue or give a suggestion?
howReport.a: Use the contact/support form, available to registered users. You can choose a type: suggestion, feature request, bug report, spam report, or other.

howReportContent.q: How do I report inappropriate content?
howReportContent.a: Every wiki page, forum post, and review has a "Report" button that automatically fills in the type as "spam" and a link to the content. Moderators will review your report.

isOpenSource.a: Yes, the entire codebase is MIT-licensed and available on GitHub. Anyone can fork the project, self-host it, or contribute code.

howContributeDev.q: How can I contribute as a developer?
howContributeDev.a: You can open an issue on GitHub or create a pull request (fork → branch → PR). CI checks lint, types, tests, and build. See CONTRIBUTING.md for local setup instructions.

howBecomeModerator.q: How can I become a moderator?
howBecomeModerator.a: If you're an active member of the community and would like to help moderate content, you can reach out to the admin team directly through the platform's contact form, or send an email to the administrators. Your contributions and activity on the platform will be reviewed before granting moderator access.

cookieBanner.text: This site uses essential cookies for authentication. No tracking cookies are used.

profile.title: My Profile
profile.name: Name
profile.exportDataDesc: Get a copy of all your data stored on this platform in JSON format.
profile.deleteAccountDesc: Permanently delete your account and all associated data. This action cannot be undone.
profile.privacyInfo: For more information about how we handle your data, see our Privacy Policy.

acceptance.title: Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.body: By accessing or using FPMI Courses, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, you may not use the platform.

account.title: Account Registration
account.body: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials. You must provide accurate and complete information when creating an account.

content.title: User Content
content.body: You retain ownership of content you submit. By submitting content, you grant the platform a license to display and distribute it. You warrant that your content does not violate any laws or third-party rights.

conduct.title: Acceptable Use
conduct.body: You agree not to misuse the platform, including but not limited to: posting spam, harassing others, uploading malicious content, or attempting to bypass security measures.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.body: The platform is provided 'as is' without warranties of any kind. The educational content is user-generated and may contain errors. Always verify information with official university sources.

changes.title: Changes to Terms
changes.body: We reserve the right to update these terms. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.

dataCollected.title: Data We Collect
dataCollected.body: We collect: account information (name, email, password hash), content you submit (posts, comments, wiki edits), and technical data (IP address, browser user agent).

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.body: We use essential HTTP-only cookies for authentication. These cookies are strictly necessary for the platform to function and do not track you across other websites. No third-party tracking cookies are used.

purpose.title: Purpose of Processing
purpose.body: Your data is processed solely to provide the platform's functionality: authentication, content management, and communication between users. We do not sell your data to third parties.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Services
thirdParty.body: We use Cloudflare Turnstile for bot detection (no tracking cookies) and Resend for transactional emails. These services process limited data under their own privacy policies.

rights.title: Your Rights (GDPR)
rights.body: You have the right to: access your data, export a copy, correct inaccuracies, delete your account, and withdraw consent at any time. Exercise these rights from your profile page.

retention.title: Data Retention
retention.body: We retain your data for as long as your account is active. Upon account deletion, your personal information is permanently removed. Public content (posts, comments) may remain anonymized.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: For privacy-related inquiries, use our contact form at /contact or reach out to the administration team.
